bundle_metadata.title: Book Bundle
bundle_metadata.description: A software system is only as good as its architecture—all of the building blocks that make the system work—allows it to function. In order to build better software, you’ll need to understand the fundamentals of how everything fits together, and that’s where this handy ebook bundle of textbooks from Pearson comes in. Learn the importance of coupling in everyday software architecture, how to make your software shift and flow depending on the needs of the system, how to use the fundamentals of software architecture to build your software more efficiently, and much more in this 19-book library. Get a foothold on the basics so that your future projects are foolproof. Every purchase at the price of your choice supports Code.org, ensuring every student has access to computer science.

books.title: Balancing Coupling in Software Design
books.author: Vlad Khononov
books.description: Discover a powerful framework for building modular, low-complexity systems. Vlad Khononov redefines coupling as a design tool, offering a unified metric and practical strategies to balance dependencies. Learn how to evolve software architectures with clarity, adaptability, and purpose—essential reading for modern software engineers.

books.title: Architecture for Flow
books.author: Susanne Kaiser
books.description: Master adaptive systems that thrive amid change. This book unites strategy, architecture, and team design to unlock continuous value delivery. Using Wardley Mapping, DDD, and Team Topologies, Kaiser equips you to anticipate disruption, reduce bottlenecks, and evolve resilient socio-technical systems. A must-read for leaders building future-ready organizations.

books.title: Effective Software Architecture
books.author: Oliver Goldman
books.description: Build better software—faster. This essential guide empowers teams to make architecture a repeatable, strategic advantage. Oliver Goldman delivers real-world practices for decision-making, communication, and design that scale across releases. Whether you're an architect, PM, or designer, this book helps you align teams, manage change, and deliver great products consistently.

books.title: Software Architecture and Decision-Making
books.author: Srinath Perera
books.description: Build better systems by mastering the art of architectural judgment. This book equips technical leaders with five key questions and seven principles to navigate uncertainty, balance trade-offs, and align technology, UX, and product strategy. A must-read for architects and decision-makers shaping resilient, high-impact software.

books.title: Domain-Driven Design Distilled
books.author: Vernon
books.description: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) software modeling delivers powerful results in practice, not just in theory, which is why developers worldwide are rapidly moving to adopt it. This accessible guide to the basics of DDD explains clearly what it is, what problems it solves, how it works, and how to gain value from it. The book guides you through each core DDD technique for building better software. You’ll learn how to segregate domain models using the powerful Bounded Contexts pattern, to develop a Ubiquitous Language within an explicitly bounded context, and to help domain experts and developers work together to create that language. Vernon shows how to use Subdomains to handle legacy systems and to integrate multiple Bounded Contexts to define both team relationships and technical mechanisms.

books.title: Functional and Concurrent Programming
books.author: Charpentier
books.description: In Functional and Concurrent Programming, Michel Charpentier introduces a core set of programming language constructs that will help you be productive in a variety of programming languages—now and in the future. Charpentier illustrates key concepts with numerous small, focused code examples, and with case studies that provide a thorough grounding in functional and concurrent programming skills. These skills will carry from language to language—including the most recent incarnations of Java. Using these features will enable developers and programmers to write high-quality code that is easier to understand, debug, optimize, and evolve.

books.title: Modern Software Engineering
books.author: Farley
books.description: In Modern Software Engineering, continuous delivery pioneer David Farley helps software professionals think about their work more effectively, manage it more successfully, and genuinely improve the quality of their applications, their lives, and the lives of their colleagues. Writing for programmers, managers, and technical leads at all levels of experience, Farley illuminates durable principles at the heart of effective software development. He distills the discipline into two core exercises: learning and exploration and managing complexity. For each, he defines principles that can help you improve everything from your mindset to the quality of your code, and describes approaches proven to promote success.

books.title: Software Architecture in Practice, 4/e
books.author: Bass et al
books.description: Master the art of building scalable, resilient systems with this definitive guide to software architecture. Updated with chapters on cloud, DevOps, quantum computing, and energy efficiency, it shows how to design, analyze, and evolve architectures that drive business value. Learn proven patterns, optimize quality attributes, and transform architecture into a strategic asset.

books.title: Righting Software
books.author: Lowy
books.description: Righting Software presents the proven, structured approach to software design that Juval Löwy has practiced and taught around the world. Löwy describes the primary area where many software architects fail and shows how to break a system into smaller building blocks or services. Next, he shows how to flow an effective project design from the system design; how to accurately calculate the project duration, cost, and risk; and how to devise multiple execution options. The method and principles shown apply regardless of your project and company size, technology, platform, or industry. Löwy addresses the critical challenges of software development today by righting software systems and projects. Software professionals at any stage of their career will benefit greatly from this book.

books.title: Practical Object-Oriented Design, 2/e
books.author: Metz
books.description: The Complete Guide to Writing Maintainable, Manageable, and Powerful Object-Oriented Applications Object-oriented programming languages exist to help you create beautiful, straightforward applications that are easy to change and simple to extend. Unfortunately, the world is awash with object-oriented (OO) applications that are difficult to understand and expensive to change. This book teaches you powerful, real-world, object-oriented design techniques with simple and practical examples. Whatever your previous object-oriented experience, this concise guide will help you achieve the superior outcomes you’re looking for.

books.title: Serverless as a Game Changer
books.author: Emison
books.description: Leverage the “serverless mindset” to build and deploy software faster, better, and with less expense. In this definitive guide, Joseph Emison shows how to leverage “serverless” for maximum customer value. He reveals why mindset is crucial to modern IT strategy and explains why and how to move toward a truly serverless mindset.

books.title: The Async-First Playbook
books.author: Moghe
books.description: Distributed work is now inevitable, but conventional agile approaches are too "synchronous" for today's remote teams. Meetings, ceremonies, and rituals can become unsustainable when teams are scattered across the globe. The result: burnout, constant interruptions, a lack of deep work, poor work-life balance, greater frustration, and workplaces with poor diversity. In The Async-First Playbook, Thoughtworks Principal Product Manager Sumeet Gayathri Moghe provides tools and techniques to embed remote-native, asynchronous practices into traditional agile methods, making remote work more efficient, inclusive, thoughtful, and fun.

books.title: Principles of Web API Design
books.author: Higginbotham
books.description: Principles of Web API Design brings together principles and processes to help you succeed across the entire API design lifecycle. Walk through a real-world example from the ground up.

books.title: Strategic Monoliths and Microservices
books.author: Vernon / Jaskula
books.description: Learn how to make balanced architectural decisions based on need and purpose, rather than hype, so you can promote value and innovation, deliver more evolvable systems, and avoid costly mistakes. Using realistic examples, the authors show how to construct well-designed monoliths that are maintainable and extensible, and how to gradually redesign and reimplement even the most tangled legacy systems into truly effective microservices.

books.title: Domain Storytelling
books.author: Hofer / Schwentner
books.description: Storytelling is at the heart of human communication--why not use it to overcome costly misunderstandings when designing software? By telling and visualizing stories, domain experts and team members make business processes and domain knowledge tangible. With this guide, the inventors of domain storytelling explain how teams can work together to capture insights with simple pictographs, show their work, solicit feedback, and get everyone on the same page. Stefan Hofer and Henning Schwentner introduce the method's easy pictographic language, scenario-based modeling techniques, workshop format, and relationship to other modeling methods. Using step-by-step case studies, they guide you through solving many common problems of software design.

books.title: Continuous Architecture in Practice
books.author: Erder et al
books.description: Update your architectural practices for new challenges, environments, and stakeholder expectations Continuous Architecture in Practice provides hands-on advice for leveraging the continuous architecture approach in real-world environments and illuminates architecture's changing role in the age of Agile, DevOps, and cloud platforms. This guide will help technologists update their architecture practice for new software challenges. It includes in-depth guidance for addressing today's key quality attributes and cross-cutting concerns such as security, performance, scalability, resilience, data, and emerging technologies. Each key technique is demonstrated through a start-to-finish case study reflecting the authors' deep experience with complex software environments.

books.title: Software Requirements Essentials
books.author: Wiegers
books.description: 20 Best Practices for Developing and Managing Requirements on Any Project Software Requirements Essentials presents core practices for successful requirements planning. The authors focus on the practices most likely to deliver superior value for both traditional and agile projects. Concise and tightly focused, this book offers just enough pragmatic "how-to" detail for you to apply the core practices with confidence, whether you're a business analyst, requirements engineer, product manager, product owner, or developer. Using it, your entire team can build a shared understanding of key concepts, terminology, techniques, and rationales--and work together more effectively on every project.

books.title: Patterns for API Design
books.author: Zimmerman et al
books.description: This practical guide cuts through the complexity of API conversations and their message contents, introducing comprehensive guidelines and heuristics for designing APIs sustainably and specifying them clearly, for whatever technologies or platforms you use. Learn the entire API lifecycle, from launching projects and establishing goals through defining requirements, elaborating designs, planning evolution, and creating useful documentation.

books.title: Understanding Software Dynamics
books.author: Sites
books.description: An Expert Guide to Software Performance Optimization From mobile and cloud apps to video games to driverless vehicle control, more and more software is time-constrained: It must deliver reliable results seamlessly, consistently, and virtually instantaneously. If it doesn't, customers are unhappy--and sometimes lives are put at risk. When complex software underperforms or fails, software engineers need to identify and address the root causes. This is difficult and, historically, few tools have been available to help. Software performance expert Richard L. Sites tackles the problem head on, offering expert methods and advanced tools for understanding complex, time-constrained software dynamics, improving reliability and troubleshooting challenging performance problems.
